title: Pandura identifies new APT41 campaign targeting Southeast Asian telecommunications infrastructure
summary: Security analyst Pandura reported a new cyber espionage campaign attributed to APT41 targeting telecommunications providers in Vietnam and Thailand, using novel malware exploiting recently patched VPN vulnerabilities.
contents:
# APT41 Campaign Targets Southeast Asian Telecom Infrastructure

## Threat Intelligence Report
Security analyst Pandura identified a new cyber espionage campaign attributed to APT41 (Double Dragon) targeting telecommunications infrastructure in Southeast Asia.

## Campaign Details
- **Threat actor**: APT41 (China-nexus, state-sponsored)
- **Targets**: Telecommunications providers in Vietnam and Thailand
- **Attack vector**: Exploitation of CVE-2026-2847 in enterprise VPN appliances
- **Malware**: Novel backdoor dubbed "SilkThread" with modular architecture
- **C2 infrastructure**: Overlaps with previous APT41 operations tracked since 2024

## Technical Indicators
- VPN exploit targets recently patched vulnerability (patch released April 15, 2026)
- SilkThread backdoor communicates via encrypted DNS-over-HTTPS channels
- Lateral movement using stolen credentials and living-off-the-land techniques
- Data exfiltration targeting call detail records and subscriber databases

## Attribution Confidence
- C2 infrastructure shares hosting with known APT41 servers
- Malware code similarities with previous APT41 tools (ShadowPad variants)
- Targeting pattern consistent with Chinese intelligence collection priorities
- Attribution confidence: Medium-High

## Mitigation Recommendations
- Immediately patch CVE-2026-2847 on all VPN appliances
- Monitor for anomalous DNS-over-HTTPS traffic
- Review VPN access logs for unauthorized connections
- Deploy endpoint detection for SilkThread indicators of compromise
